aggregators.name: cross_tool_lru_regime
aggregators.purpose: Reports the LRU-vs-GRASP sub-WSS vs post-WSS regime inversion across all 3 tools (does LRU climb out of the deficit once the working-set spills L3?).

aggregators.name: cross_tool_saturation
aggregators.purpose: Reports the doubly-saturated cell census: which (graph, app, L3) tuples have BOTH cache_sim AND anchor (gem5 or sniper) collapsing all policies to within sat_floor_pp.

aggregators.name: cross_tool_slope_ordering
aggregators.purpose: Reports the strict per-tool slope ordering (POPT > GRASP > SRRIP > LRU at the corpus-median level) across all 3 tools.

aggregators.name: cross_tool_slope_universality
aggregators.purpose: Reports whether the steepness-band universality claim (every policy's POPT-vs-LRU steepness lies in the same band across cache_sim/gem5/sniper) holds today.

aggregators.name: cross_tool_winners
aggregators.purpose: Reports the per-cell winner-policy across cache_sim/gem5/sniper (does GRASP win in cache_sim AND in sniper, or is it split?).

aggregators.name: anchor_cross_tool_agreement
aggregators.purpose: Reports the gem5-vs-sniper anchor-cell agreement: for the shared anchor cells, does sniper's POPT slope and gem5's POPT slope agree in sign and magnitude within thresholds?

rules.S1: every CROSS_TOOL_AGGREGATORS entry's artifact exists on disk
rules.S2: every artifact is valid JSON with a top-level object
rules.S3: every declared top-level key is present in the on-disk JSON
rules.S4: every declared evidence path resolves to a non-empty list/dict
rules.S5: every declared cell-row required-key is present in every evidence row
rules.S6: every declared tools-path value is a subset of canonical ['cache_sim', 'cache-sim', 'gem5', 'gem5-riscv', 'gem5-x86', 'sniper', 'sniper-sift']
rules.S7: every declared verdict-path value has the declared type
